title: CircleCI の 503 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article provides a comprehensive overview of 503 errors in the context of CircleCI, covering common causes and actionable solutions.
Most factual claims are well-supported by official CircleCI documentation, status pages, and general HTTP error definitions.
The troubleshooting steps and recommendations align with industry best practices for CI/CD pipeline management.
A few claims are plausible inferences or general best practices rather than direct, explicit statements from primary sources, slightly reducing the factual score and citation coverage.

sources.title: 503 Service Unavailable - HTTP - MDN Web Docs
sources.claim: The HTTP 503 Service Unavailable server error response status code indicates that the server is not ready to handle the request.

sources.title: 503 Service Unavailable: Meaning, Causes, Fixes - ClickMinded
sources.claim: 503 means the server is temporarily unavailable. It is always server-side, never your fault as a visitor, and almost always fixable.

sources.title: HTTP Error 503 Service Unavailable and How to Fix it? - Scrapfly Blog
sources.claim: HTTP error 503 Service Unavailable is a response status code indicating that the server cannot handle the request at the moment. This is usually temporary and often occurs when the server is undergoing maintenance or experiencing a sudden spike in traffic.

sources.title: What is HTTP Error 503 and How to Fix It - Network Solutions
sources.claim: Error 503 (Service Unavailable) means that the website's server is temporarily unable to handle incoming requests. This usually happens due to heavy traffic or ongoing maintenance.

sources.title: 503 Service Unavailable - HTTP status code explained
sources.claim: 503 means the server is temporarily unavailable for all clients (overload, maintenance).

sources.title: Provisioning Service returned status code: 503 - Feedback & Bug Reports - CircleCI Discuss
sources.claim: Our entire pipeline is stuck as none of our builds pass.

sources.title: An update on CircleCI's reliability
sources.claim: As the heart of our customers' delivery pipelines, we know that when we go down, your ability to ship grinds to a halt as well.

sources.title: CircleCI Status
sources.claim: Scheduled Maintenance — Bitbucket Integration.

sources.title: Circle CI Status. Check if Circle CI is down or having an outage. | StatusGator
sources.claim: Circle CI posts separate notifications for planned maintenance work.

sources.title: Pricing and Plan Information - CircleCI
sources.claim: Credits are the currency of CircleCI. Credits are purchased up-front and are used to pay for CircleCI platform usage in real time.

sources.title: Using credits - CircleCI Docs
sources.claim: Credits are used to pay for users and usage based on machine type, size, and features such as Docker Layer Caching.

sources.title: CircleCI Review: Features, Pricing, Pros & Cons (2026) | Siit
sources.claim: CircleCI uses a credit-based pricing model that scales with team size and usage.

sources.title: What is CircleCI? A Look at Features and Use Cases | Harness Blog
sources.claim: Credits are bundled in credit packages and their value per unit of time depends on the compute instance that is chosen to run the client's jobs.

sources.title: Free Plan - CircleCI Docs
sources.claim: The Free Plan offers five active users.

sources.title: Config best practices: concurrency and parallelism - CircleCI
sources.claim: CircleCI's free plan provides a concurrency limit of 30, meaning you can run up to 30 tasks at the same time.

sources.title: Have a better build and test with CircleCI with 4 concurrent jobs [#3242196] | Drupal.org
sources.claim: You will get up to 4 concurrent jobs available. Only on Linux-medium resources.

sources.title: Artifacts download error " 503 Service Unavailable: Back-end server is at capacity"
sources.claim: 503 Service Unavailable: Back-end server is at capacity.

sources.title: Is Circle CI Down? Check current status and user reports | IsDown
sources.claim: When Circle CI goes down, incidents typically resolve within 139 minutes based on historical data.

sources.title: CircleCI reduced outages after their promise to do so. We proved it with data. - Reddit
sources.claim: I can say that CircleCI came very close to keeping its promises -- they decreased outage duration by 41% per month on average.

sources.title: Automatic reruns - CircleCI Docs
sources.claim: Automatic reruns provide a safety net for your CI/CD pipelines by automatically retrying failed steps and/or workflows.

sources.title: Automatic retry of workflows released - CircleCI Changelog
sources.claim: You can now specify max-auto-reruns in your workflow configuration with a value between 1 and 5 to automatically rerun failed workflows.

sources.title: Introducing Automatic Reruns! - Build Environment - CircleCI Discuss
sources.claim: We have added automatic workflow retry functionality to help you handle flaky resources and intermittent failures without manual intervention.

sources.title: How to Retry a Failed Step with "when" Attribute? - CircleCI Support
sources.claim: Note: CircleCI does not support automatic retries of failed steps, jobs & workflows.

sources.title: Trigger CircleCI pipelines from your IDE with natural language
sources.claim: Sometimes you need to trigger a build manually—to test pipeline changes, retry a flaky test, or run CI on a colleague's branch—without the friction of pushing empty commits or navigating to the UI to manually trigger a build.

sources.title: Pipelines overview and setup - CircleCI Docs
sources.claim: Pipelines can be triggered in the following ways: Automatically: Git events, scheduled events, custom webhooks. Manually: CircleCI app, or via the API or CLI.

sources.title: Users, organizations, and integrations guide - CircleCI Docs
sources.claim: An organization in CircleCI is a workspace that serves as a container for your team's projects, settings, permissions, and billing. Organizations are typically linked to your version control system (VCS) account(s) (GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket).

sources.title: CircleCI Support Center
sources.claim: CircleCI provides support for all your questions related to building and running applications on our platform.

sources.title: How to submit a support ticket - CircleCI Support
sources.claim: To submit a support ticket on CircleCI and receive assistance with the issues you are facing, please follow the steps below:

sources.title: CircleCI Support Plans
sources.claim: Our team of experts is here to guide you from proof of concept, to launch, to build optimization. And when issues arise, our team will get you back to green, faster.

sources.title: Build a CircleCI dashboard to visualize all your CI/CD data - SquaredUp
sources.claim: To roll up the status of all your production-level CircleCI pipelines to a wallboard or single pane of glass.

sources.title: Using Insights - CircleCI Docs
sources.claim: The CircleCI Insights dashboard provides an overview of the health and usage of your projects' build processes.

sources.title: Security overview - CircleCI Docs
sources.claim: Use restricted contexts with teams to share environment variables with a select security group.

sources.title: New CircleCI features for secure secrets management
sources.claim: Users can request logs of system activity for any 30-day period in the previous 365 days by visiting Organization Settings > Security and selecting a date range.

sources.title: Restrict your pipeline traffic to specific IP ranges - CircleCI Docs
sources.claim: IP ranges is a feature for CircleCI customers who need to configure IP-based access to their restricted environments using existing workflows and platforms.

sources.title: IP Address Ranges for Safelisting/Do You Have Static IP Addresses Available?
sources.claim: IP ranges let you limit inbound connections to your infrastructure to IP address ranges that are verifiably associated with CircleCI.

sources.title: Troubleshoot - CircleCI Docs
sources.claim: In the CircleCI application, check the individual job and workflow views for error messages. More often than not, the error is because of formatting errors in your .circleci/config.yml file.

sources.title: Troubleshooting Common Errors in Runner Logs - CircleCI Support
sources.claim: When reviewing your Runner logs, pay special attention to these HTTP status codes: 500: Internal Server Error; 404: Not Found; 403: Forbidden; 429: Too Many Requests.

unsupported_claims: クレジット残量が不足している場合、ジョブがキューに入ったまま実行されず、その結果として 503 エラーが返却されることがあります。(If credit balance is insufficient, jobs remain in the queue without execution, potentially returning a 503 error.)
CircleCI公式SDKやライブラリを使用している場合は、自動リトライ機能が組み込まれている可能性があるため、ドキュメントを確認してください。(If using official CircleCI SDKs or libraries, automatic retry features might be built-in, so check the documentation.)
Free プランはリソース共有型であり、ピーク時間帯に 503 エラーが発生しやすくなります。(The Free plan is resource-sharing and 503 errors are more likely during peak hours.)
improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.

Optional improvement: Provide a more direct citation from CircleCI documentation that explicitly links credit insufficiency or free plan concurrency limits to the occurrence of 503 errors, if available.
Optional improvement: If possible, verify whether CircleCI's official SDKs or libraries specifically include automatic retry functionality for API calls.

Optional improvement: Ensure all example code snippets are syntactically correct and follow CircleCI's recommended practices (though this is outside the scope of grounding, it affects overall publication quality).
Optional improvement: Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
Optional improvement: Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.
